reasoning: With the tragic passing of Kelvin Kiptum (the world record holder at 2:00:35 and the only runner seriously positioned to break the 2-hour mark in the near term) in February 2024, the likelihood of another runner breaking the 2-hour barrier legally before the end of 2025 is exceptionally low. Eliud Kipchoge is past his prime, and the remaining top marathoners have personal bests that are well over a minute slower than Kiptum's record, and nearly 2 minutes away from a sub-2 hour time. Breaking the 2-hour barrier legally requires an unprecedented leap in performance which usually takes a runner multiple years of steady, world-leading progression. Given the forecast date in late 2025 and the timeline of major marathons, the probability of a legal sub-2 occurring before December 31, 2025, is practically negligible.
